summary:
Bone resistance to compression mainly comes from its mineral content, particularly due to high concentrations of calcium compounds which maintain structural integrity under pressure. This aligns with multiple sources highlighting the critical function of these minerals in sustaining bones' ability to withstand mechanical stress without fracturing or deforming significantly during normal activities such as walking or standing upright where weight-bearing occurs frequently through skeletal structures including long limbs and vertebrae among other bony prominences subjected regularly but intermittently depending upon posture shifts etc... To reiterate succinctly though : Mineral composition provides essential rigidity needed preventing excessive deformation when compressed thus ensuring overall stability & health across various body parts bearing loads daily via movement patterns involving dynamic interactions between muscles tendons ligaments fascia skin alongwith underlying osseous framework itself being most directly impacted here regarding direct contact load transmission dynamics occurring continuously even at rest state too albeit lesser magnitude compared active phases...
To summarize concisely while retaining core information: Resistance to bone compression stems fundamentally from its robust mineral structure rich in calcified components providing necessary stiffness; this characteristic ensures adequate support amidst applied pressures encountered normally during physical activity enhancing longevity durability minimizing risk fractures amongst numerous benefits conferred thereby...
